Find bare line breaks inside fields

Source: R/utils.R
bare_lf_positions.Rd

CRIS records are terminated by CRLF; a bare LF (not preceded by CR) is field content — typically a multi-line address — that breaks the row for every CSV parser. Returns positions of such LF bytes in `chunk`. Quote state is deliberately not tracked: CRIS data contains stray literal quotes in unquoted fields, which make quote-parity unreliable, and legal quoted line breaks in a CRLF file use CRLF (which this leaves alone).

Usage

bare_lf_positions(chunk, prev_byte)

Arguments

chunk

Raw vector of file bytes

prev_byte

Last byte of the preceding chunk

Value

Integer positions within `chunk`
